title: bidens campylotheca subsp. waihoiensis
intro: Bidens campylotheca subsp. waihoiensis is a rare and endemic plant species found in New Zealand, specifically in the South Island. It is a subspecies of the Bidens campylotheca plant, also known as the 'giant beggar ticks'. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to its high content of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Bidens campylotheca subsp. waihoiensis include its potential use as a treatment for various diseases, including cancer and cardiovascular disorders, and it has been traditionally used to prepare herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and extracts for medicinal purposes.
